def: A metre. It has two lines, each of 31 instants. Each line has nine feet, with pause at the 10th and 18th instants. Thus, (4 x 2) +2, 4+4, (4+3)+1. Kellogg .

direct plural; oblique/vocative singular

def: s.m. Confusion, jumble, mess; error, discrepancy (in an account); complication; — entanglement; — deception, duplicity, fraud; — an unlawful attachment, a liaison

def: s.m. House, dwelling, mansion, habitation, abode, home; — a room (in a house), an apartment; — a den; nest; hole; sty; cell; burrow; — a receptacle; frame; stand; cup; pan; case; holder; socket; groove; channel; — a drawer; till; pigeon-hole (of a desk, &c.); a compartment or division; partition; — room, space; — a square or cell (of a chess-board, almanac, &c.); a loop, loop-hole; button-hole; — station, seat, region, quarter; — source, spring, mine, root, parent; place of origination or production, native place
more: n. m. S. गृह Pālī, geham, geho , Pr. ghara . 1. A house ( khānah ). 2. A dwelling; habitation; mansion. 1. Ghar meṅ nahīṅ būr, beṭā māṅge motī-chūr . Prov. Not even bran in the house, and the son and heir wants lollypops! 2. Bāhar miyāṅ sūbedār, ghar meṅ bīvī jhokeṅ bhāṛ . Prov. My lord's abroad a captain gay, his wife at home the fire feeds all the day. 3. Ghar āë kutte ko bhī nahīṅ mārte haiṅ . Prov. We do not drive away even a dog that comes to our house (for refuge). 4. Āṭe kā chirāg ghar rakkhūṅ to chūhā khāë, bāhar rakkhūṅ to kavvā le jāë . Prov. A corn-flour lamp inside the house by rats will eaten be, keep it outside by crows it's snapped up speedily. 5. Jo yārī jorego, chhorā, do ek dinā ko tau nā tyāgūṅgī ghar ko balmā, Jo yārī jorego, chhorā, sadā madā ko tyāg dūṅgī ghar ko balmā, Yārī joṛego to rākhūṅgī gale ko ḍhulnā . Rus. Wom. song. If you 'll but be my love, boy! Just for…

n. m. Cont. of گهورَا A horse. ghuṛ-bahal , n. m. A vehicle drawn by a horse. ghuṛ-chaṛhā , n. m. 1. A mounted soldier (Sikhs). 2. A horseman; trooper. ghuṛ-chaṛhī , n. f. 1. The bridegroom riding on horseback. This ceremony is observed on the way to the bride's house with the nuptial procession. 2. Cavalry; field battery. ghuṛ-dauṛ , n. f. 1. A horse race. 2. A race-course. ghuṛ-dauṛ kā ghoṛā , n. m. A race horse. ghuṛ-sāl , A. astabal , n. m. A stable. ghuṛ-sem , n. f. The horse bean. ghuṛ-nāl , A cannon. ghur-makkhī , n. f. A horse-fly. ghuṛ-muṅhā , adj. Horse-faced.

To or in every house, from house to house. گهربيٿهنا ghar baiṭhnā, To be ruined. Mīr Dard says: جس سمت کوتوني آنکهہ اٿهاکر ديکها مانند حباب گهرکي گهربيٿهہ گئي Jis simt ko tū ne ānkh uṭhākar dekhā, mānand-i-ḥubāb ghar ke ghar baiṭh gaʼe, Wherever you have turned your eyes, whole families, like bubbles of water, have vanished. گهرجانا ghar jānā, To be ruined. گهرچلانا ghar chalānā, To provide for oneʼs household expenses. (Beng.). گهردار ghar-dār, s. m. (dakh.) Household, housekeeping. گهردواري ghar-dwārī, s. f. A kind of tax, poll-tax, hearth-money. گهرڐبونا ghar ḍubonā, To ruin oneʼs self and family. گهرڐوبنا ghar ḍūbnā, To be ruined. گهر کا دهيلانا رهنا ghar kā dhelānā rahnā (dakh. i. q. ويران هونا) To become, or to be, desolate. گهر کرنا g…

To bring ruin on a house

To stick in the house, to keep much at home (cf. ghar-basā)

v. a. Caus. of گهرَنا q. v. 1. Rāmnagar meṅ gahnā gaṛhavloṅ, Arrah meṅ le gaile chor, Je Rām dehalin, se hī Rām lehalin, kachhu na bigṛal mor . Bhoj. Wom. song. wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit *𑀖𑀭𑀸𑀬𑀬𑀡 (*gharāyayaṇa) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀓- (-ka-) from Sanskrit *घरायतन (*gharāyatana), from *घर (*ghara) + आयतन (āyátana), in which *घर (*ghara) is a reborrowed Prakrit form of गृह (gṛhá) (or गृध (gṛdha), which is from Proto-Indo-Iranian *gʰr̥dʰás, from Proto-Indo-European *gʰr̥dʰós, from *gʰerdʰ- (“to enclose”). Cognate with Punjabi ਘਰਾਨਾ (gharānā) / گھرانہ (ghrānh), S…

def: part. adj. & s.f. = ghaṛī, q.v. s.f. A ravine; a valley (see ghār); — a cattle-shed; — a calamity.
more: n. f. S. घटी 1. A space of 24 minutes. 1. Ghaṛī meṅ ghaṛiyāl hai . Prov. In the hour bell each one's death knell. 2. Burī ghaṛī na āve . Prov. Saying. May evil hour never come! 2. An instrument for measuring time; a watch. 3. A subdivision of a village. ghaṛī bhar rahnā , ( Slang ) To plead for one half hour with a harlot. ghaṛī bhar meṅ , adv. In a moment; in a trice. ghaṛī ṭhīk k. , v. a. To regulate a watch. ghaṛī-sāz , H. P. n. m. A watchmaker. ghaṛī sāat kā h. , v. n. To be on the point of death. ghaṛī sāf k. , v. a. To clean a watch. ghaṛī kūknā , v. a. To wind a watch. ghaṛī ghaṛī , adv. Repeatedly; again and again; often. achchhī ghaṛī, subh yā nek ghaṛī , n. f. An auspicious moment. burī ghaṛī , n. f. Bad time; inauspicious moment. dharam ghaṛī , ( Slang ) A fundament from which great guns are let off. dhūp-ghaṛī , n. f. A sun dial. ret-ghaṛī , n. f. An hour glass.

def: s.f. The space of twenty-four minutes; — an hour; — time, hour; a small (indefinite) period of time, a moment; an instrument for measuring time; a watch, clock, &c.; a gong; — a subdivision of a village

def: v.t. = gaṛhnā, or ghaṛnā, q.v. v.t. (dialec.) = gāṛnā, q.v. v.t. To strain, filter; — to squeeze, press; to milk.
more: Bhoj. v. a. 1. To make; forge; coin; work (metals). Ghaṛe kumhār, bhare sansār . Prov. The potter makes, the world fills. (Pitchers). 2. To malleate; mould; form; figure; shape. Zevar ghaṛnā . 3. To beat; thrash ( ṭhoṅknā 2). 4. To invent; make up (a story). Jau sasur—baiṭho, baitho! aisī jhūṅṭhī sāṅchi tukeṅ ghattu hai! Such lies as this rascal (father-in-law) sits and makes up there! ghaṛ denā , v. a. To make up; frame. Pālnā ghaṛ de re, baṛhaiyā ke, agar chandan kā pālnā re, Jhūleṅge Kuaṅr Kanhāi! Song. Ṭhumrī . A cradle, carpenter, make me, of fragrant Sandal wood and Agar it must be, the Prince Kanhaiyā shall a rocking lie! ghaṛ'ant, ghaṛat , n. f. 1. Making; workmanship. 2. Beating; hammering. 3. Fabrication; invention. Gharant kā ustād hai .
v. a. To form, malleate. See H. گڙهنا gaṛhnā.

Jāte hī ghaṛvā meṅ aisī āg lagāvegī kī dhur se lagāvegī aur utāregī . Wom. Saying. No sooner she arrives she 'll raise a flame, a spark set here she 'll draw down there.

def: s.f. The having (a thing) made, &c.; making, fashioning; — the cost of making ( = ghaṛāʼī, q.v.).
s.m. (f. -ī), See s.v. ghar. s.m. Land worn away by running water; a ravine; clay soil in low situations (where rain-water lies for a time).

s.f. Grass; hay; straw; fodder
more: n. f. S. घास Grass; hay. 1. Ghās khā, ghās! (Said by shopkeepers to a customer who wants to buy cheap). 2. Kaisī banī re, Har Dās! ghoṛī dānā khāë na ghās . Prov. 3. Ghās khudāvan maiṅ chalī, saṅg chalī merī sās, Sauk napātī yū kahe, 'chalī dhagaṛ ke pās! ' Wom. Some grass to cut I went, my mother-in-law with me, My co-wife, wretch, says 'she her lover's gone to see!' ghās-pāt , n. m. Greens. ghās phūs , n. m. Straw; weeds. ghās kā gaṭṭhā . A bundle of grass; pottle of hay. ghās khānā , v. a. 1. To live on grass. 2. To eat hemp. 3. To lose one's wits or senses. ghās khodnā, yā kāṭnā , v. a. To cut grass. Paṛhte ho, yā ghās khodte ho! ghās kī murgī , n. f. Grass Fowl or partridge. ghasyārā , Contemp. ghas-khudā , n. m. A grasscut. ghās-vālī , n. f. A woman who sells or cuts grass.
s. f. Grass, straw, hay, fodder. گهاس پات ghās-pāt, Sweepings.

To grub up grass (with a khurpā or hoe)

def: s.f. Proper or suitable time or period (for a work or action), opportune moment, opportunity; — design, intention, aim; — cover, concealment, ambush, ambuscade, trap, snare; enmity, treachery
more: n. f. S. हन् 1. See دانو 2, 4. 2. Killing; slaughter. Iskī bagal meṅ kattī, teg uske hāth meṅ hai, Yeh uskī fikr meṅ hai, wo iskī ghāt meṅ hai . Nazīr. Stuck in this one's side a knife, and grasps a sword another, Bent on murder this one stands, in ambush lies the other. ghāt k. , v. a. To kill; destroy. ghāt meṅ baiṭhnā yā rahnā, ghāt k. , v. n. To lie in ambush; lurk; waylay.

n. m. S. घट्ट 1. Cut; shape ( tarāsh ). 2. A ferry (اتارا 5). 3. A bathing place on the bank of a river; a dyke; quay. 4. A place for washing clothes on the river side. 5. The curve in the blade of a sword. 6. A bride's skirt. 7. Pearl barley. ghāṭ bārī . 1. Lands attached to a ferry. 2. The duty or toll levied at a ghāṭ. ghāṭ kaptān , n. m. A harbour master; a master attendant at a sea-port. ghāṭ ghāṭ kā pānī pīnā , v. n. 1. To see the world. 2. To acquire large experience. ghāṭ-mārā, ghāṭ-mār , n. m. 1. One who evades payment of a toll or duty. 2. A smuggler ( chaukī-mār ). ghāṭ mārnā , To smuggle dutiable goods ( chaukī mārnā ). ghāṭ māṅjhī , n. m. A ferryman. One who supplies and regulates the hire of boats, provides the crews, and exercises general superintendence. ghaṭ-vāliyā, ghaṭ-vāl, ghāṭiyā , n. m. A Brahman who attends where pilgrims bathe, and supplies sandal, etc. for certai…
adj. Inferior; less. 1. Woh kyā tum se kuchh ghāṭ hai? Wom. 2. Tāne ghāṭ, ki bāne ghāṭ ? Is the warp or the woof defective? (Both equal or alike). Prov.
s. f. Aim, design, snare, ambuscade, ambush, opportunity. گهات تاکنا ghāt-tāknā, To watch an opportunity. گهات کرنا ghāt karnā, To waylay, to lie in ambush.
s. Killing, slaughter. گهات کرنا ghāt karnā, To kill.
s. m. A landing place; a quay, wharf, ferry, ford, pass, bathing place on a river side. گهاٿمار ghāṭ-mār, s. m. A smuggler. گهاٿ مارنا ghāṭ-mārnā, To smuggle.

Less; inferior; abated; defective

def: n. m. 1. Contraction of ghāṭ , q. v. 2. Mind ( dil ). 1. Issar ghaṭ ghaṭ bāsī hai . God is in every thing. 2. Ghaṭ māṅ dhiyān dhagaṛ kā lāve, Parghaṭ tiryā Har gun gāve . Rus. Her secret thoughts are on her lover bent, While outwardly on worship she's intent. ghaṭ meṅ baiṭhnā , v. n. To dwell in the heart.
Hand to hand, congression, shock, conflict, encountering, congressus.

ety: ghaṭ˚ = Prk. घट्ट(इ) = S. घट्टय(ते), pass. of rt. घट्ट)
def: v.n. To lessen, diminish, decrease, waste, dwindle, shrink, contract; to abate, decline, subside, go down; to fall; to fail; — to become allayed or appeased, &c.; to become deficient; to be deducted or subtracted (from, -se); to be spent; to be or become abortive; to be depressed; to be compressed
v. n. See kam h. , and ghaṭānā. ghaṭy'al , adj. 1. Inferior; coarse. 2. Cheap. 3. Base; low-born.
more: n. m. The knee. 1. Āgejāveṅ to ghuṭneṭūṭeṅ, pīchhe dekheṅ to āṅkheṅ phūteṅ! An oath. May you break your legs if you go forward and be struck blind if you look back! 2. Khushī raho, jijmān, nain tere donoṅ phūṭeṅ! Ghuṭnoṅ ke bal gire, dāṅt battīsoṅ ṭūṭeṅ! Go prosper, my constituent, may both your eyes be pent! May you on all-fours crawl, your two and thirty teeth out fall! ghuṭnoṅ meṅ sir de lenā , v. n. To hang down one's head (with shame, grief, or sulks). ghuṭne se lagī baiṭhī rahnā , Wom. v. n. To stick to one's apron. ghuṭniyoṅ chalnā, ghuṭnoṅ ke bal chalnā , To crawl. 1. Tumhārā laṛkā ghuṭniyon kab chalegā ? Prov. When will your child crawl on his knees? (When will you speak the truth, or perform your promise). 2. Shah-zor apne zor meṅ girtā hai ān-kar , Dard. Woh tift kyā giregā jo ghuṭnoṅ ke bal chale . The strong man who strives mightily must heavy fall, No such mishap the crawli…

v. n. 1. To be pounded, ground. 2. To be well-boiled or cooked. Gal gaī, ghuṭī nahīṅ . 3. To be polished, furbished. 4. To be shaved clean; to undergo tonsure. 5. To be choked, suffocated. Galā yā dam ghuṭnā . 6. To fill. Sāre makān meṅ dhuāṅ ghuṭ gayā . 7. To be engaged in conversation. 8. To be close friends. ghuṭ'an , n. f. 1. Sultriness. 2. Suffocation.

To be surrounded. chāṅdnī gherā , ( Ast .) The zodiacal light.

def: v.t. To surround, encompass; to encircle; infold, inclose, take in, include, contain; to fill; to occupy; — to fence, to hedge or rail or wall in; to beset; to hem in; to lay siege to, to invest, besiege, blockade
more: v. a. 1. To surround; compass; enfold; encircle; encompass. Nazīr! āṅdkī meṅ kahte haiṅ ki aksar deo kote haiṅ: Miyāṅ! ham ko to le jātī haiṅ pariyāṅ gher āṅdhī meṅ . Nazīr. Nazīr! in sterms they say that demons num'rous fill the air. But I, good Sir! in storms am borne away by paris fair. 2. To enclose; fence; hedge in; rail in; wall in; hem in. 3. To besiege; blockade; invest. 1. Rāstah ghernā . 2. Shikār ghernā . 4. To keep a mistress; marry a widow. kākā! kāhie gher le nā ? Rus. Why dont you keep a woman in the house, Uncle? 5. To comprehend; include. 6. To fill; occupy. Jagah ghernā . 7. To follow; fawn; flatter. Do mahīne kalakṭar ko gherā, jab yeh naukrī milī After two months waiting on the Collector I got this post.
v. a. To surround, compass, encircle, fence, invest, beset, blockade. گهيرناگهارنا ghernā ghārnā, To surround, &c.

A base one sit not with! it is an evil thing, Now thick as peas, anon a venomed snake to sting. ghī ke jalnā , v. n. lit . to be lighted (lamps fed with ghī in place of oil). To gain one's object; to be successful. Dushmanoṅ ke to ghī ke jal gaë. ghī ke kuppe se jā lagnā , v. n. lit . to cling to a keg of butter. To find a mine of wealth. wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit *𑀖𑀭𑀸𑀬𑀬𑀡 (*gharāyayaṇa) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀓- (-ka-) from Sanskrit *घरायतन (*gharāyatana), from *घर (*ghara) + आयतन (āyátana), in which *घर (*ghara) is a reborrowed Prakrit form of गृह (gṛhá) (or गृध (gṛdha), which is from Proto-Indo-Iranian *gʰr̥dʰás, from Proto-Indo-European *gʰr̥dʰós, from *gʰerdʰ- (“to enclose”). Cognate with Punjabi ਘਰਾਨਾ (gharānā) / گھرانہ (ghrānh), S…

def: s.f. Smell, disagreeable smell, stink.
more: n. f. S. घ्राण 1. Smell. 2. (Bhoj. ghināb ) Stink.

def: s. (dakh.) Lizard, chameleon ( Harris ): see گرگٿ girgiṭ.

ety: ghir˚ = gher˚, see ghernā and gherā+kā = S. क+कः
def: s.m. A round-about, a whirligig (a short pole is fixed in an upright position in the ground, and another is placed horizontally across it on a pivot, and this latter pole is made to whirl round by two boys sitting one at each end of it); — the game so played.
more: n. m. Children sitting at either end of a beam which is made to turn round a pole.

s. (dakh.) A game among children: a little pole being fixed in the ground with another across resting on a pivot, two boys, one sitting at each end of the horizontal pole, with their feet touching the ground, whirl round ( Qanoon-e-islam, app. p. lvii).

def: v.n. To be surrounded, be encompassed (by, -se), be enclosed; to be collected around; — to gather (as clouds, &c.)
more: v. n. 1. See ghernā . 2. To gather (as clouds).
v. n. 1. To be surrounded, enclosed, to be collected around. 2. To gather (as, the clouds).

ety: prob. fr. ghirnā, q.v.; or corr. of ghurnī, q.v.
def: s.f. A pulley; a wheel for twisting ropes; — a kind of pigeon, the tumbler; — a species of kingfisher, Ceryle rudis
more: n. f. 1. A pulley. 2. A wheel for twisting ropes ( garrī , 1). 3. A vertigo; swimming in the head; giddiness. 4. Ceryle rudis ; the black and white kingfisher. Found along all rivers, and so called from the precipitous way he dashes down from a height into the water after his prey. It breeds in holes in sand-banks made by himself, eggs white, season February, March, and April. ghirniyoṅ kī jot , ( Mech .) A system of pulleys. jamī ghirnī . ( Mech .) A fixed pulley. chaltī ghirnī . ( Mech .) A moveable pulley.

s. f. 1. A pulley. 2. An instrument for twisting ropes. 3. A kind of pigeon, the tumbler. ¶ ghirnī khānā, To go round. ¶ ghurnī (from घूर्ण ) s. f. A vertigo, swimming in the head, rolling. ¶ gharnī (for गृहिणी ) s. f. A wife.
subs: v.n. To go round and round, to whirl, to revolve; to tumble (as a pigeon)

def: v.n. To go rubbing along; to move with friction or rubbing; — to rub along; to drag along; — to last long (as shoes, &c.)
subs: (-kī), To rub (particularly, to rub the forehead of one who has a headache with a cowrie, in order to afford relief)
'To be not enough for a rub'; (fig.) to be scarce
s.f. A boys' game (rubbing one string upon another to see which will first be worn through).

ety: perf. part. of ghisnā; — and = Prk. घसिअओ = S. घर्षित+कः
def: part. adj. Rubbed; abraded; worn smooth (as a coin, &c.)
v.n. To go rubbing along; to move with friction or rubbing; — to rub along; to drag along; — to last long (as shoes, &c.)
more: n. m. S. घृष to rub. 1. Friction; abration. Uskā kankavvā ek hī ghisse meṅ kāṭā . 2. ( Met .) ( ghasā ) A son. Kyoṅ be ūt ke ghase! māntä nahīṅ! Slang. ghissā lagnā , v. n. To be rubbed, abraded. gissā-mīrī , An encounter in cutting one string with another (Boys); sawing a string.
subs: (-kī), To rub (particularly, to rub the forehead of one who has a headache with a cowrie, in order to afford relief)
'To be not enough for a rub'; (fig.) to be scarce
s.f. A boys' game (rubbing one string upon another to see which will first be worn through).

ety: caus. of ghisnā or ghasnā; — ghasā˚ = ghasāw˚ = Prk. घसावे˚ = S. घर्ष+आपि caus. aug.
def: v.t. To cause to rub; — to abrade, to wear (by rubbing).
more: v. a. Caus. of گهسنا ghisāv'aṭ, ghisāo , n. Rubbing; friction; attrition; abrasion.
(causal of گهسنا ghisnā ) v. a. To cause to rub, to abrade, wear.

ety: ghisā(nā) + āʼo = Prk. एअव्वं = S. (इ)तव्यं; — and ˚āwaṭ = Prk. एअव्वअट्टं = S. (इ)तव्य+क+त्वं
def: Rubbing; friction; attrition; abrasion.
more: s. m. (from گهسنا ghisnā ) Attrition, rubbing, friction, abrasion.

def: v. n. S. घर्षण. To be dragged along; to trail. Koī ghisaṭtā zamīn ke ūpar, koī khushī se falak-nashīn hai, Yeh bhed apnā voh āphī jāne, kisī ko hargiz khabar nahīṅ hai . Nazīr. Some drag along the ground, some soar elate to Heaven, To know the cause is His, 't is not to mortals given. ghisṭe ghisṭe phirnā , v. n. To be dragged about from place to place.

ety: ghisā(nā) + āʼo = Prk. एअव्वं = S. (इ)तव्यं; — and ˚āwaṭ = Prk. एअव्वअट्टं = S. (इ)तव्य+क+त्वं
def: Rubbing; friction; attrition; abrasion.
more: s. f. (from گهسنا ghisnā ) Attrition, rubbing, friction, abrasion.

ety: ghisiyā˚ = ghisyā˚ = S. घर्षित (p.p.p. of rt. घृष्)+ā = āw = Prk. āwe = S. āpi, caus. augment
def: v.t. To trail, to drag.
more: (r. घृष Rub) v. a. To trail, to drag.

ety: ghisak, prob. fr. S. घर्ष+कृ+ī = S. इका
def: s.f. (dialec.) Rubbing; friction; — a slight rub, a graze.

ety: Prk. घसणअं or घिसणअं; S. घर्षणीयं, rt. घृष्
def: v.t. To rub, to rub off or away, to abrade; to make sore by rubbing, to gall; — to rub down, to groom (a horse); — to whet, to grind down; — to beat, bruise; to harm, injure
more: v. n. S. घर्षण. Pālī, ghamsatī . 1. To be rubbed, abraded. 2. To be worn away. Tum likh doge to tumhāre kyā hāth ghis jāëṅge? ghisnā , v. a. See رگرَنا Beṭā, thoṛo chandan ghisau hanāë dhoë baiṭhe hau! ghisā huā, ghisā , adj. Worn out (coin, etc.). ghis ghis-kar chalnā , v. n. To last long (as shoes, etc.). ghis lagāne ko na h. , v. n. To be scarce. Āj kal ghī ghis lagāne ko to hai hī nahīṅ .
1. v. n. To be rubbed, to be abraded, to be worn, to fret, wear. 2. v. a. To rub, to rub off, whet. 3. To beat. 4. adj. Apt to be worn by rubbing.
subs: v.n. To be rubbed; to be abraded; — to be worn
(by rubbing or friction); to be rubbed smooth (as a coin, &c.); — to waste away, to wear, to fret; — s.m. Rubbing; wearing away, &c.; — harming, injuring; — adj. Liable (or apt) to be worn by rubbing

def: s.f. Rubbing, &c.; — adv. By rubbing
more: adv. By rubbing; as, گنڙ گهسني کرنا or گهسني چلنا gaṅṛ ghisnī karnā or ghisnī chalnā, To move along, rubbing on the ground, as a child on his buttocks before he is able to walk.
subs: v.n. To move along, rubbing on the ground (as a child on his buttocks before he is able to walk).

def: (dakh.) Arable land ( Harris ).

s.m. = ghī, q.v. s.m. Clarified butter (or butter which has been boiled gently and strained and allowed to cool; it is much used in cooking, and is highly esteemed by Hindūs both in food and for religious uses)
subs: (-meṅ), To rub ghī (on a cake of bread)

adj. & s.m.f. Like ghī and khićaṛī; intimate, close (friends), hand and glove; — close or intimate friends; — ghī-kā jalnā, 'The burning of ghī' (in a lamp, in place of oil); — (met.) to be well off; to be successful; to attain one's object
'To go and stick to a ghī-bottle': (met.) to come in for a fortune; to find a mine of wealth
ghī gayā par kuppā to gayā nāhīṅ
prov. 'The ghī may be gone, but the bottle remains' (used in hinting to one who is making some false assertion, that, though the matter be past, all documents, &c. respecting it are not lost)

ety: S. घृत+पूर+कः; or Prk. घिअउल्लओ = S. घृत+उल+कः
def: s.m. A small kind of cucumber (much used as a condiment).

ety: ghī, q.v.+wālī, fem. of wālā, or S. आलः, with euphon. w
def: adj. Containing much ghī; rich (milk).
adj. Like ghī; — soft; tender; — s.m. A pompion, Cucurbita lagenaria
more: n. m. A vegetable; Cucurbita lagenaria , Speede. ghīyā torī , n. f. Luffa pentandra , Wat. ghīyā-kas ; Ped. ghīyā-kash . See kaddū-kash. lambā ghīyā, laukī . Bottle-gourd. mīṭhā ghīyā , n. m. Sweet pumpkin ( sītā-phal ).
s. m. Name of a vegetable, a pumpion. (Cucurbita lagenaria). گهيا توري ghiyā toraʼī, Luffa pentandra: Roxb.
subs: or ghīyā-torī, s.f. The Luffa pentandra
s.m. An instrument for cutting and cleaning pumpkins (syn. kaddū-kash)

def: adj. Containing or yielding much ghī (milk); rich (milk).
s. f. Aliment.
adj. & s.m. (used as last member of compounds), Striking; killing, destroying; removing; — killer, murderer, destroyer, &c.

s.m. (dialec.) = گهوڙا ghoṛā, q.v. adj. (f. -ā), Awful, terrific, frightful, terrible, horrible, dreadful; violent, vehement; — deep (as colour, or as sleep); — s.m. The terrible, an epithet of Śiva; — awfulness, horribleness; — a horrible action; — horror; trepidation; dread, awe; — sound or roll (of a drum); — roar; thunder; — dirt
subs: (˚ra+āk˚), adj. & s.m. or ghorākr̤iti (˚ra+āk˚), adj. & s.f. = ghor-rūp, q.v.
adj. (f. -ā), Of horrible or frightful appearance; terrifying, terrific
adj. (f. -ā), Of terrible aspect or form; frightful, hideous, grim; — s.m. A hideous appearance
v.n. To conceal (one's) dislike or indignation
s.m. An awful (or a terrifying) sound
s.m. Tartarus; hell with all its horrors.

def: s.m. 1˚ = ghoṅghā; — 2˚ = ghoghī, qq.v. s.m. A cockle; cockle-shell, Cochlea helix; a snail; snail-shell; any spiral univalve shell (syn. āʼīṅṭhā).
s.f. = ghoṅghī or ghūṅgī, and ghūngaṭ, qq.v. s.m. A cockle; cockle-shell, Cochlea helix; a snail; snail-shell; any spiral univalve shell (syn. āʼīṅṭhā).
more: s. f. 1. A pocket, pouch, wallet. 2. A turtledove.

def: v.t. = ghoshnā, q.v. v.t. To speak or utter aloud; to proclaim; — to repeat, recite (prayers, or a lesson, &c.); to iterate.
v.t. = ghoshnā, q.v. v.t. To speak or utter aloud; to proclaim; — to repeat, recite (prayers, or a lesson, &c.); to iterate.
more: v. a. S. घुष To repeat a lesson frequently in order to learn it by heart.

def: s.m. (Dakh.) = gokhrū, q.v.
s.m. Buttermilk (syn. ćhāćh).
more: s. (dakh.) Purple, garden or small purslain. (Portulaca oleracea, Lin. See Qanoon-e-islam, app. p. xxxix).

def: s.f. (dialec.) Purslane, Portulaca oleracea; — a kind of reddish-white sea-fish ( = gholā).

ety: ghola, rt. of gholnā, q.v.+S. कः; — or perf. part. of gholnā
def: s.m. An intoxicating potion made of opium or bhaṅg; — a mixture; solution; — a kind of reddish-white sea-fish
more: s. m. 1. An intoxicating potion made of bhang or opium. 2. Name of a fish. گهولي مين ڐالنا ghole meṅ ḍālnā, To involve in difficulty.
subs: (-ko), To throw into confusion or disorder; to involve in difficulty; to harass, worry, injure.

ety: perf. part. of gholnā, and = Prk. घोलिअओ; S. घोलित+कः, a Prk. form of घूर्णितः
def: part. adj. (f. -ī), Mixed; stirred, stirred up; disturbed; — thick; turbid
adj. 1. Turbulent, seditious, vicious. 2. Stout, fat, round.
v.t. = ghulānā, q.v. v.t. To melt, dissolve; to mix (in or with, -meṅ); to cause to waste away; — (dialec.) to coax, wheedle, allure, entice, seduce, deceive.
more: v. a. (dakh. i. q. فريب دينا) To deceive, allure, entice.

ety: rts. of gholnā and ghumāʼonā = ghumānā, qq.v.
def: s.m. Evasion; subterfuge; procrastination.
s. m. Mixture, mixing.
s.m. A mixing; mixture.

ety: ghol˚ = Prk. घोल(इ) = S. घोलय (ति), rt. घोल्, a Prk. form of घूर्न्
def: v.t. To mix (with a liquid); to dissolve, melt; to stir together, stir up; to shake about, to agitate briskly.
more: v. a. S. घोल to mix. To melt; dissolve.
(caus. of گهلنا ghulnā गलन ) v. a. To mix with a liquid, to dissolve.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀖𑁄𑀮𑁂𑀤𑀺 (gholedi), from Sanskrit घोलयति (gholayati), perhaps related to घूर्णति (ghū́rṇati).

ety: ghol(nā) + wā = āwā = Prk. एअव्वअं = S. (इ)तव्य+कं
def: s.m. A solution; mixture; gruel; slops; — an intoxicating drink made of opium ( = gholā, q.v.)
more: n. m. 1. A mixture. 2. Gruel; slops. 3. An intoxicating drink made of opium. gholvā gholnā , v. a. 1. To make too thin or watery. Gholvā ghol-ke rakh detī hai . 2. To delay ( der lagānā ). 3. To injure one.
subs: To make a watery mess (of a thing), make too thin; — to practise evasion; to procrastinate, to delay; — to reduce to a semi-fiuid state; to do injury (to), to injure (a person).

def: s.m. A cockle; cockle-shell, Cochlea helix; a snail; snail-shell; any spiral univalve shell (syn. āʼīṅṭhā).
more: n. m. S. कम्बुक 1. A snail shell; any spiral univalve shell. Bhare samandar, ghoṅgā hāth . Prov. An ocean without bound, a sea-shell only found! 2. A species of fresh-water snail. 3. A cockle or cockle shell ( Cochlea helix ).
(perhaps, from s. घूर्ण Turning round) s. m. A cockle, or cockle-shell, a snail. (Cochlea helix; also, called āʼīnthā. See شامک shāmuk ).

def: s.m. = ghoṅgā, q.v. s.m. A cockle; cockle-shell, Cochlea helix; a snail; snail-shell; any spiral univalve shell (syn. āʼīṅṭhā).
s.f. dim. of ghoṅghā = ghoṅgā, q.v. s.m. A cockle; cockle-shell, Cochlea helix; a snail; snail-shell; any spiral univalve shell (syn. āʼīṅṭhā).
